athlete.notes: Home training. Weights are per dumbbell. Adjust in chat with Claude anytime. Knock knees (mild, noted Jul 2026) → cue knees OUT on every squat/lunge/step-up, and keep glute-med work (side leg raises, single-leg bridges) in every week. Left knee: mild discomfort on twists (noted Jul 2026) — knee work stays in pain-free range, no knee caving inward, no loaded twisting. Desk job (sits all day) → hip-flexor stretch every cooldown. Goal: start jogging — building calf capacity now; add a Saturday walk-jog day after 2-4 knee-quiet weeks. Sessions use supersets (blocks sharing a group interleave sets).

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
Warm-up pace
PUSH KNEES OUT as you stand — the key cue for knock knees
Hands on a chair seat — the higher the hands, the easier. Build here before going to the floor
Per leg — hips level, knee pointing straight up. Direct work against knock knees
Per arm, brace on chair
Slow 3-sec lowering — building toward jogging
Cooldown, one set per leg
Cooldown, one set per leg — opens hips after desk sitting

full-body-b.title: Full Body B — Lunge & Pull
equipment: Sturdy chair (rows, bench dips, seated calf raises)

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
10 per leg — step BACK, front shin vertical, knee tracks over the middle toe (don't let it drift inward)
On the mat — lie down, back fully supported
Per side, slow — side-glute work that pulls the knees back out into line
Per arm, brace your free hand on the chair — supported, so it spares the lower back
Use a sturdy chair — bend your knees and keep feet close for an easier version
Per leg — rest the dumbbell on your thigh just ABOVE the knee, never on the kneecap
Light weight, strict form
Slow — lower back stays pressed into the mat. Replaces Russian twists while your back settles
Cooldown, one set per leg — opens hips after desk sitting

full-body-c.title: Full Body C — Hinge & Carry
equipment: Clear space at the stairs (step-ups + incline push-ups)

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
Bottom stair, 10 per leg — push through the heel and keep the knee from caving inward
Hands on the stairs, slightly wider than shoulders — pick a step height where 8 reps are clean
Hinge at hips, soft knees, flat back — skip this one if your back is complaining
Sit on chair or bench
Slow down-phase — when 3x15 feels easy, tell Claude to progress to single-leg
Slow and controlled
One set per side
Cooldown, one set per leg — opens hips after desk sitting
